# Pixiedust Knowledge Wiki

> Agent-readable public index for Pixiedust knowledge packs and concepts.
> Raw Markdown for any page: /pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/<path>.

## Start here

- Root pack HTML: /pixi-wiki/root.html
- Root pack raw Markdown: /pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/llms.txt
- JSON document registry: /pixi-wiki/index.json
- Full public corpus: /pixi-wiki/llms-full.txt
- Human HTML surface: /pixi-wiki/

## Scope

Covers: Pixiedust agent knowledge routing, Knowledge concepts, project entrypoints, MOCs, and KPR operating boundaries.

Not covered: private vault content outside the generated public surface, secrets, raw memory/Honcho data, runtime automation internals, MCP/search/RAG/confidence calibration, or facts newer than source dates.

## Domains

### Knowledge

Entrypoints:
- [Knowledge Domain Pack](/pixi-wiki/knowledge.html) — role: `domain-entrypoint` — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/llms.txt` — current: 2026-06-15

Concepts:
- [AI-Native Problem Framing Framework](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-ai-native-problem-framing-framework.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/ai-native-problem-framing-framework.md` — updated: 2026-06-11
- [Agent Capability Route Pattern](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-agent-capability-route-pattern.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/agent-capability-route-pattern.md` — updated: 2026-06-13
- [Agent Wikis](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-agent-wikis.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/agent-wikis.md` — updated: 2026-06-15 — Agent-native knowledge surfaces that give agents maintained, scoped, source-backed maps before they act.
- [Bounded Context Tree Pattern](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-bounded-context-tree-pattern.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/bounded-context-tree-pattern.md` — updated: 2026-06-07
- [Building Software Is Learning](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-building-software-is-learning.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/building-software-is-learning.md` — updated: 2026-06-11
- [Find the Lock: Problem-First Approach](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-find-the-lock-problem-first.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/find-the-lock-problem-first.md` — updated: 2026-06-11
- [Hermes SOUL.md Wiring](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-hermes-soul-md-wiring.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/hermes-soul-md-wiring.md` — updated: 2026-06-12
- [Local Retrieval-Augmented Agent Infrastructure](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-local-retrieval-agent-infrastructure.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/local-retrieval-agent-infrastructure.md` — updated: 2026-06-15 — Applied architecture lessons from Krill's dual-RTX 5090 local agent stack — hardware setup, retrieval pipeline (Qdrant + Ollama + MCP), agent workflow governance, and relevance to Jamie's PC build decisions.
- [MOC Knowledge Cortex](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-moc-knowledge-cortex.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/moc-knowledge-cortex.md` — updated: 2026-06-13 — Model of the MOC layer as the routing brain of the vault, keeping durable notes reachable through clear domain branches.
- [Matt Pocock SDLC Rhythm](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-matt-pocock-sdlc-rhythm.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/matt-pocock-sdlc-rhythm.md` — updated: 2026-06-07
- [Peer Profiles vs Child Processes](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-peer-profiles-vs-child-processes.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/peer-profiles-vs-child-processes.md` — updated: 2026-06-13 — Architecture decision for when Jamie's crew agents run as peer Hermes profiles versus child/subagent processes.
- [Profile Memory Boundaries](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-profile-memory-boundaries.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/profile-memory-boundaries.md` — updated: 2026-06-13 — Boundary model for routing durable knowledge between USER.md, Hermes memory, local knowledge, Obsidian/GitHub project truth, and skills.
- [Runtime Memory Knowledge Routing](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-runtime-memory-knowledge-routing.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/runtime-memory-knowledge-routing.md` — updated: 2026-06-15 — Runtime rule for combining injected memory, Honcho, local Hermes knowledge, Obsidian Knowledge, skills, GitHub/project truth, and session search without turning memory into a wiki dump.
- [Self-Improving Agent Systems](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-self-improving-agent-systems.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/self-improving-agent-systems.md` — updated: 2026-06-13 — Model for agent systems that improve through verified durable state, evidence grades, promotion ladders, and future-run retrieval rather than weight updates.
- [Verb-First Product Positioning](/pixi-wiki/concept-knowledge-concepts-verb-first-product-positioning.html) — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Knowledge/concepts/verb-first-product-positioning.md` — updated: 2026-06-11

### Maps of Content

Entrypoints:

- [Hermes Mission Control Pack](/pixi-wiki/projects-hermes-mission-control.html) — role: `project-entrypoint` — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Projects/Hermes Mission Control/llms.txt` — current: 2026-06-15
- [Planned Program Intel Pack](/pixi-wiki/projects-planned-program-intel.html) — role: `project-entrypoint` — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Projects/Planned Program Intel/llms.txt` — current: 2026-06-15
- [Projects Domain Pack](/pixi-wiki/projects.html) — role: `domain-entrypoint` — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/Projects/llms.txt` — current: 2026-06-15

### Vault Root

Entrypoints:
- [ObsidianVault Root Pack](/pixi-wiki/root.html) — role: `root-entrypoint` — raw: `/pixi-wiki/pixi-wiki/raw/llms.txt` — current: 2026-06-15


<!-- ===== Knowledge/llms.txt ===== -->

---
title: "Knowledge Domain Pack"
type: knowledge-pack
status: active
current_as: 2026-06-15
confidence: high
scope:
  covers:
    - Obsidian Knowledge wiki routing
    - Schema, index, log, raw-source, and synthesized-page obligations
    - Boundary between Obsidian Knowledge, local Hermes knowledge, memory/Honcho, projects, and skills
  not_covered:
    - Local Hermes knowledge files under ~/.hermes/knowledge
    - Memory/Honcho retrieval internals
    - Project execution state
    - Broad schema rewrites or mass migrations
freshness:
  policy: on_change
  stale_after_days: 30
owners:
  routing: Pixoid
  maintenance: Quill
  source_inventory: Boba optional
sources:
  - label: Knowledge Schema
    path_or_url: Knowledge/SCHEMA.md
    role: contract
  - label: Knowledge Index
    path_or_url: Knowledge/index.md
    role: catalog
  - label: Knowledge Log
    path_or_url: Knowledge/log.md
    role: maintenance-log
  - label: Runtime Memory Knowledge Routing
    path_or_url: Knowledge/concepts/runtime-memory-knowledge-routing.md
    role: concept
  - label: Knowledge Agent Instructions
    path_or_url: Knowledge/AGENTS.md
    role: operating-rule
  - label: Knowledge MOC
    path_or_url: Maps of Content/Knowledge MOC.md
    role: human-navigation
human_surface:
  - path_or_url: Maps of Content/Knowledge MOC.md
  - path_or_url: Knowledge/index.md
  - path_or_url: Knowledge/SCHEMA.md
fallback_rules:
  - If a concept/entity/query is missing from the index, search `Knowledge/` before creating a page.
  - If the content is project-specific state, route to Projects domain and GitHub/project hubs.
  - If the content is a procedure, route to skills.
  - If the content is a tiny stable user/profile fact, route to memory/USER/Honcho.
  - If the content is agent-operational and not human-facing, check `~/.hermes/knowledge/llms.txt`.
v_next:
  - static_html
  - automated_freshness_check
---

# Knowledge Domain Pack — Agent Entrypoint

## Agent Start Here

Use this pack after the root `/llms.txt` when working with the Obsidian Knowledge wiki. This pack routes agents to schema/index/log/source obligations. It is routing truth, not canonical truth.

## What This Pack Routes

- `Knowledge/SCHEMA.md` — operating contract and OKF-compatible canonical-note rules.
- `Knowledge/index.md` — catalog of concepts, entities, comparisons, queries, and meta pages.
- `Knowledge/log.md` — append-only maintenance trail.
- `Knowledge/AGENTS.md` — short local editing instructions.
- `Knowledge/concepts/`, `entities/`, `comparisons/`, `queries/`, `_meta/`, and `raw/`.
- Boundaries between Obsidian Knowledge, local Hermes knowledge, memory/Honcho, project truth, and skills.

## What This Pack Does Not Route

- Live project implementation or issue state.
- Local Hermes knowledge under `~/.hermes/knowledge/` except as a fallback/pointer.
- Memory/Honcho internals or transcript archaeology.
- Broad schema migrations or mass frontmatter rewrites.
- Runtime Hermes profile, gateway, cron, provider, or webhook mutation.

## Source-of-Truth Hierarchy

1. **Knowledge/SCHEMA.md** — wiki contract, frontmatter rules, page patterns, and ingest/query/lint workflows.
2. **Knowledge/index.md** — current catalog and first read for queries.
3. **Knowledge/log.md** — maintenance history.
4. **Synthesized Knowledge pages** — reusable human-facing concepts/entities/queries/comparisons.
5. **Knowledge/raw/** — immutable source evidence leaves.
6. **Obsidian project/GitHub truth** — project-specific state.
7. **Hermes local knowledge** — agent-operational reusable concepts.
8. **Skills** — repeatable procedures.
9. **Memory/Honcho/USER** — compact stable facts and preferences.
10. **This pack** — routing truth.

## Canonical Sources

| Label | Path/URL | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Schema | `Knowledge/SCHEMA.md` | contract |
| Index | `Knowledge/index.md` | catalog |
| Log | `Knowledge/log.md` | maintenance log |
| Agent instructions | `Knowledge/AGENTS.md` | operating rule |
| Runtime Memory Knowledge Routing | `Knowledge/concepts/runtime-memory-knowledge-routing.md` | concept |
| Knowledge MOC | `Maps of Content/Knowledge MOC.md` | human navigation |

## Current Narrative / Operating Posture

Obsidian Knowledge is Jamie's human-facing durable knowledge wiki for reusable concepts, entities, comparisons, and substantial queries. It mirrors some local Hermes knowledge concepts when useful, but it is not the same layer as `~/.hermes/knowledge/` and not a replacement for memory/Honcho.

The `agent-wikis` concept preserves the broader pattern: maintained, scoped, source-backed knowledge surfaces for agents. Knowledge Pack Routing is the current Pixiedust implementation.

## Domain Map

| Area | Use | First source |
|---|---|---|
| `Knowledge/concepts/` | reusable frameworks/lenses | `Knowledge/index.md` |
| `Knowledge/entities/` | people/orgs/tools/projects/regulators | `Knowledge/index.md` |
| `Knowledge/comparisons/` | side-by-side durable synthesis | `Knowledge/index.md` |
| `Knowledge/queries/` | substantial answered questions | `Knowledge/index.md` |
| `Knowledge/raw/` | immutable source material | parent synthesized page or `Knowledge/raw/README.md` |
| `Knowledge/_meta/` | audits, decisions, lint/meta notes | `Knowledge/index.md` |

## Do Not Do

- Do not edit files under `Knowledge/raw/` after ingest.
- Do not create pages for passing mentions.
- Do not put project execution state here when it belongs in GitHub/project hubs.
- Do not put long concept essays in memory.
- Do not create procedures here when they belong in skills.
- Do not mass-migrate or rewrite schema/frontmatter from this pack alone.

## Fallback Rules

1. Query existing Knowledge: read `Knowledge/index.md`, then search `Knowledge/`.
2. Creating/updating a Knowledge page: read `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, recent `log.md`, and search for existing pages first.
3. After a content change: update the page, refresh `index.md`, append `log.md`, and update Knowledge MOC only if retrieval path changed.
4. Project-specific fact: route to Projects domain and project/GitHub truth.
5. Agent-operational reusable concept: check `~/.hermes/knowledge/llms.txt` and local Hermes knowledge before duplicating.

## Human Surface

- `Maps of Content/Knowledge MOC.md`
- `Knowledge/index.md`
- `Knowledge/SCHEMA.md`

## Maintenance Notes

Update this pack when the Knowledge schema, index/log workflow, domain folders, or cross-layer boundary rules change. Keep it compact; detailed knowledge belongs in pages and schema, not this routing pack.

    - Template content or note generation rules
    - Runtime automation or static HTML publishing

  - path_or_url: Maps of Content/Knowledge MOC.md
fallback_rules:
  - If a task needs canonical project truth, route to `Projects/llms.txt` and GitHub/project hubs.
  - If a task needs reusable concepts, route to `Knowledge/llms.txt` and local Hermes knowledge.
  - If a task needs root vault orientation, route to `llms.txt`.
  - If a template is needed, use `Templates/README.md`; no Templates pack exists yet.
v_next:
  - static_html
  - automated_freshness_check
---

# Maps of Content Domain Pack — Agent Entrypoint

## Agent Start Here

Use this pack when working on MOCs themselves or when deciding whether a human navigation surface needs maintenance. MOCs are human navigation surfaces. `llms.txt` files are agent retrieval contracts. Do not collapse one into the other.

## What This Pack Routes

- Which MOC to inspect for vault navigation.
- How agents should maintain MOCs without treating them as canonical truth.
- Boundaries between MOCs, root/project/knowledge packs, project hubs, and GitHub.
- The current decision that `Templates/llms.txt` is deferred unless template-routing rules become distinct enough to justify it.

## What This Pack Does Not Route

- Current project implementation state.
- Full Knowledge wiki content.
- Template body content.
- Asset/evidence handling under `Attachments/`.
- Runtime automation, cron, MCP, RAG, crawler, search, vector DB, or public hosting.

## Source-of-Truth Hierarchy

1. **Canonical domain sources** — GitHub/project hubs/Knowledge schema/raw sources, depending on the topic.
2. **Domain packs** — root, Projects, Knowledge, and project-specific `llms.txt` files.
3. **MOCs** — human navigation and cross-link surfaces.
4. **This pack** — routing truth for MOC maintenance boundaries.

## Canonical Sources

| Label | Path/URL | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Top-level MOC | `Maps of Content/Maps of Content.md` | human navigation |
| Projects MOC | `Maps of Content/Projects MOC.md` | project navigation |
| Knowledge MOC | `Maps of Content/Knowledge MOC.md` | knowledge navigation |
| Agent Operations MOC | `Maps of Content/Agent Operations MOC.md` | agent-ops navigation |
| MOC Maintenance Process | `Maps of Content/MOC Maintenance Process.md` | maintenance |
| Tag Index | `Maps of Content/Tag Index.md` | tag navigation |

## Current Narrative / Operating Posture

The vault now has a small agent entrypoint mesh: root, Projects, Knowledge, project-specific packs, and this MOC domain pack. MOCs remain the human map layer. Agents should use MOCs to orient and discover, then verify against canonical domain sources before acting.

Templates currently have a compact `Templates/README.md` and do not need a separate `Templates/llms.txt`. Create one later only if template selection/maintenance becomes a recurring agent routing problem.

## Do Not Do

- Do not treat MOC text as proof of current project state.
- Do not duplicate full project or Knowledge indexes inside this pack.
- Do not create one pack per folder by default.
- Do not create `Templates/llms.txt` just because the folder exists.
- Do not perform broad vault reorganization from this pack.

## Fallback Rules

1. Need a project: root pack → Projects pack → project-specific pack or project `Index.md` → GitHub.
2. Need a reusable concept: root pack → Knowledge pack → Knowledge index/schema → local Hermes knowledge if agent-operational.
3. Need human navigation maintenance: this pack → relevant MOC → canonical domain source.
4. Need a template: use `Templates/README.md`; stop and create a Templates pack only if distinct routing rules are necessary.

## Human Surface

- `Maps of Content/Maps of Content.md`
- `Maps of Content/Projects MOC.md`
- `Maps of Content/Knowledge MOC.md`
- `Maps of Content/Agent Operations MOC.md`

## Maintenance Notes

Update this pack when MOC structure changes, when a new domain MOC becomes central to routing, or when Templates/Attachments/Daily Notes receive their own pack decision. Keep this as a routing surface, not a duplicate vault index.

    path_or_url: https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm/issues/54
    role: implementation

  - path_or_url: Project Board.md
fallback_rules:
  - If stale, verify live issue/PRD/repo state before acting.
  - If asked about old switch-point efficacy, route to parked diagnostics and do not treat as current proof.
  - If asked for real wildfire/policy claims, refuse as out of scope.
v_next:
  - static_html
  - automated_freshness_check
---

# Critical Ranger FFM Pack — Zone-Control RL MVP

## Agent Start Here

Use this pack before Critical Ranger work. Active roadmap: zone-control RL MVP. Parked path: switch-point/single-cell efficacy, preserved only as diagnostic/prototype infrastructure. Next executable slice is the zone-control action contract fixtures issue.

## What This Pack Routes

- Current zone-control RL MVP roadmap.
- Parked/superseded switch-point efficacy work.
- Active repo, PRD, parent tracker, and next issue.
- Evidence gates, non-claims, and anti-cheat constraints.
- VPS vs local WSL execution guardrails.

## What This Pack Does Not Route

- Real wildfire validity.
- Policy or operational wildfire recommendations.
- GPU/Puffer/training/render commands on VPS.
- Local WSL gates without Jamie approving exact commands.
- Old switch-point path as final science proof.

## Source-of-Truth Hierarchy

1. **GitHub issues/PRs** — coordination truth.
2. **Zone-control PRD** — requirements truth.
3. **Repo code/tests** — implementation truth.
4. **Obsidian project Index** — durable synthesis.
5. **This pack** — routing truth.

## Canonical Sources

| Label | Path/URL | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Project hub | `Projects/Critical Ranger FFM/Index.md` | synthesis |
| Repo | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm` | implementation |
| Zone-control PRD | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm/blob/main/docs/PRD-zone-control-rl-mvp.md` | requirement |
| Parent tracker | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm/issues/53` | coordination |
| Next issue | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/critical-ranger-ffm/issues/54` | implementation |

## Current Narrative / Operating Posture

The main roadmap is zone-control RL MVP: test whether an RL agent can reduce mega-fires in a toy forest-fire model better than honest baselines without clearing the forest or over-spending interventions. The prior single-cell switch-point efficacy work is parked as diagnostic/prototype infrastructure.

Next gate: issue #54 action contract fixtures — deterministic zone indexing, no-op, one-zone thinning, and decision interval semantics. Do not jump to training or rendering.

## Do Not Do

- Do not run GPU/Puffer/training/render/raylib/c_render work on the VPS.
- Do not run local WSL HITL commands without Jamie approving the exact command.
- Do not claim final efficacy, SOC-control, publication-grade science, policy value, or real-wildfire validity.
- Do not delete parked switch-point artifacts.
- Do not implement from parent tracker alone; follow child issue scope.
- Do not overwrite README.md or docs/PRD.md without explicit instruction.

## Fallback Rules

1. If stale (>14 days), re-verify issue #53, issue #54, current PRD, and repo state.
2. If question touches old efficacy path, route to parked diagnostics and label it parked.
3. If question asks for real-world wildfire claims, refuse as out of scope.
4. If no match, fall back to Project Board then Projects MOC.

## Human Surface

- `Projects/Critical Ranger FFM/Index.md`
- `Project Board.md`
- `Maps of Content/Projects MOC.md`

## Maintenance Notes

Update when issues #54-#59 close, when a PRD changes, when main roadmap pivots, or when Jamie approves a HITL local run. Confidence is medium until post-#54 implementation facts are refreshed.

  - If stale, verify live cron and issue/PR state before acting.
  - Outbound persona delivery is not inbound execution proof.
  - Repo/test harness proof is not deployed always-on operation.
v_next:
  - static_html
  - automated_freshness_check
---

# Hermes Mission Control Pack — Agent-Ops Coordination Hub

## Agent Start Here

Use this pack before crew-ops work: cron, gateway, delivery, profile routing, capability routing, or Pixoid/Crew coordination. Verify live runtime state before changing anything. This pack routes; it does not authorize mutations.

## What This Pack Routes

- Mission Control project hub and Agent Operations MOC.
- Cron governance and heartbeat/noise policy.
- Delivery routing and persona webhook architecture.
- Default Pixoid gateway/control-plane vs worker profiles (`tinker`, `quill`, `boba`; `pika` parked/future).
- Capability Registry / Reuse Gate and agent-ops health surfaces.
- Failure modes: rate limits, delivery failure, GitHub auth failure, silent-marker suppression.

## What This Pack Does Not Route

- Secrets, tokens, webhook URLs, or credentials.
- Profile/gateway/cron mutation without an approved issue.
- Project-specific build work outside Mission Control.
- Full VPS/Docker setup detail.

## Source-of-Truth Hierarchy

1. **Live runtime checks** for cron/profile/gateway state when action depends on runtime.
2. **GitHub issues/PRs** — coordination truth.
3. **Mission Control Index** — durable crew-ops synthesis.
4. **Agent Operations MOC** — human navigation.
5. **Pixoid Setup** — infrastructure truth.
6. **Skills** — procedural truth.
7. **Session search** — archaeology only.
8. **This pack** — routing truth.

## Canonical Sources

| Label | Path/URL | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Mission Control Index | `Projects/Hermes Mission Control/Index.md` | coordination |
| Agent Operations MOC | `Maps of Content/Agent Operations MOC.md` | synthesis |
| Pixoid Setup | `Projects/Personal/Pixoid Setup.md` | architecture |
| Capability Routes | `Projects/Pixoid Agent Capability Routes/Index.md` | architecture |
| Vault issue tracker | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/ObsidianVault/issues` | coordination |

## Current Narrative / Operating Posture

Mission Control is the crew-ops hub. Agent-ops harness, reuse gate, capability registry, persona webhook migration, harmonization, Knowledge Pack Routing V1, stack-level workflow integration, and integration QA are complete. KPR is now wired as a retrieval-first layer for Pixoid/Quill/Tinker/Boba workflows. KPR V2 PRD review is accepted and tracker issues exist: #74 parent, #75 static HTML human surface, and #76 non-mutating freshness report. No V2 implementation has started; the next gate is explicit Jamie/Pixoid routing of exactly one child issue, with #76 recommended first. Jamie also identified a broader vault-wide entrypoint mesh question; #77 tracks that as design only and must stay separate from #74/#75/#76. Planned Program Intel is the first consumed project pack — proof, not primary goal. Deck/Loom/application work is optional portfolio packaging.

Pixoid is the default Hermes control plane and Discord gateway. Worker profiles are bounded by route contracts. Outbound webhooks give personas voice/identity; they do not prove live inbound execution.

## Do Not Do

- Do not print or store secrets, webhook URLs, tokens, or credential material.
- Do not mutate profiles, gateways, webhooks, cron jobs, or provider configs without explicit issue approval.
- Do not claim outbound delivery as inbound route proof.
- Do not claim a repo-level test harness is a deployed operational route.
- Do not start worker gateways without separate identity/route configuration.
- Do not implement #75, #76, or #77 from this pack alone; require explicit routing.

## Fallback Rules

1. If stale (>7 days), run live checks before acting: `hermes cron list --all`, relevant issue/PR state, and profile/gateway state if needed.
2. Infrastructure question: route to `Projects/Personal/Pixoid Setup.md`.
3. Route-governance question: route to `Projects/Pixoid Agent Capability Routes/Index.md`.
4. Skill/procedure question: load the relevant Hermes skill.
5. Secrets request: refuse and do not derive or echo.

## Human Surface

- `Projects/Hermes Mission Control/Index.md`
- `Maps of Content/Agent Operations MOC.md`
- `Project Board.md`

## Maintenance Notes

Update after cron schedule changes, route matrix changes, profile/gateway changes, capability registry changes, or crew composition changes. Use elevated Quill model for major source-of-truth rewrites; cheap model is acceptable for small freshness updates.

# Planned Program Intel Pack — Active Portfolio Project

## Agent Start Here

This pack routes the active Planned portfolio/application project. Active repo: `pixiiidust/planned-program-intel`. Historical predecessor: `pixiiidust/planned-program-intelligence`. This pack is KPR V1's first proof-of-consumption project pack, not the primary system goal. Current Planned work, if Jamie re-prioritizes it, is packaging the completed demo into deck, Loom, and application materials — not continuing app build work.

## What This Pack Routes

- Portfolio/application narrative.
- Active repo and live demo routing.
- Decision-routing and institutional-memory vocabulary.
- The six-question decision moment.
- Next packaging work: slide deck, Loom walkthrough, application submission.

## What This Pack Does Not Route

- Old `planned-program-intelligence` build issues or PDLC scoring as current framing.
- New app-build work unless Jamie explicitly reopens it.
- General event-planning operations outside the demo's decision-intelligence layer.

## Source-of-Truth Hierarchy

1. **GitHub issues/PRs on `planned-program-intel`** — coordination truth.
2. **`CONTEXT.md`** — product language truth.
3. **`docs/prd/2026-06-11-real-build-six-slices.md`** — requirements truth.
4. **`prototype/NOTES.md`** — prototype findings.
5. **Obsidian project Index** — durable synthesis.
6. **Live demo** — current product surface.
7. **`Projects/Planned/Index.md`** — historical reference only.
8. **Root `llms.txt`** — vault routing context.

## Canonical Sources

| Label | Path/URL | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Active repo | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/planned-program-intel` | implementation |
| Live demo | `https://pixiiidust.github.io/planned-program-intel/` | implementation |
| Language reference | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/planned-program-intel/blob/main/CONTEXT.md` | architecture |
| PRD | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/planned-program-intel/blob/main/docs/prd/2026-06-11-real-build-six-slices.md` | requirement |
| Prototype notes | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/planned-program-intel/blob/main/prototype/NOTES.md` | synthesis |
| Obsidian project hub | `Projects/Planned Program Intel/Index.md` | synthesis |
| Historical predecessor | `Projects/Planned/Index.md` | historical |

## Current Narrative / Operating Posture

Build queue is complete. The demo is live. The project now needs a crisp portfolio/application story: problem, decision-routing solution, inspectable evidence, compounding program memory, and roadmap.

Core language:

- **Decision** — human resolution needed for an event program.
- **Resolution** — accept/change/override/escalate plus reasoning.
- **Owner** — best customer-side decision maker.
- **Evidence** — cases, exceptions, and precedents.
- **Program Memory** — accumulated cases, patterns, exceptions, and precedents across events.

KPR V1, stack-level integration, and integration QA are closed. Planned is the first proof/project pack demonstrating KPR consumption, not the main goal. Deck/Loom/application packaging remains optional portfolio work if Jamie re-prioritizes it.

## Do Not Do

- Do not describe this as a chatbot, autonomous agent system, or RL product.
- Do not resume old `planned-program-intelligence` issues unless Jamie explicitly asks.
- Do not restart app-build slices by default; packaging is the current job.
- Do not describe the old repo or old GitHub Pages URL as current live demo.
- Do not use PDLC scoring as the primary current frame.

## Fallback Rules

1. Active repo unreachable: use the Obsidian Index and state that repo state needs re-verification.
2. Question about old prototype: route to `Projects/Planned/Index.md`; label it historical.
3. Pack stale: verify live demo HTTP status, active repo issue state, and current project index before claims.
4. Question about external Planned.com product context: route to Boba/web research, not this pack alone.

## Human Surface

- `Projects/Planned Program Intel/Index.md`
- `Maps of Content/Projects MOC.md`
- `Portfolio.md`
- `Projects/Planned/Index.md` for historical predecessor only

## Maintenance Notes

Update after deck creation, Loom script/final recording, application submission, or any new PR/issue on `planned-program-intel`. Keep this pack portfolio-first and execution-second.

# Projects Domain Pack — Agent Entrypoint

## Agent Start Here

Use this pack after the root `/llms.txt` when the task is project-related but before choosing a specific project hub. This pack routes agents through Jamie's project bounded contexts. It is routing truth, not canonical truth.

## What This Pack Routes

- Active project contexts.
- Parked and historical project contexts.
- Project-specific knowledge-pack coverage.
- Which project surfaces are canonical for current state.
- Proof-project drift guardrails.

## What This Pack Does Not Route

- Detailed code implementation steps.
- Live repo branch/PR truth without GitHub verification.
- Runtime Hermes profile/gateway/cron mutations.
- Personal secrets, credentials, or private env details.

## Source-of-Truth Hierarchy

1. **GitHub issues/PRs** — coordination and implementation truth.
2. **Project PRDs/ADRs** — requirements and architecture truth.
3. **Project `Index.md` notes** — durable project synthesis and boundaries.
4. **Project-specific `llms.txt` packs** — routing truth for projects that have them.
5. **Project Board** — current queue and milestone navigation.
6. **Projects MOC** — human project map.
7. **Skills** — procedures.
8. **Session search/web** — fallback/archaeology only.
9. **This pack** — project-domain routing truth.

## Canonical Sources

| Label | Path/URL | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Projects MOC | `Maps of Content/Projects MOC.md` | synthesis |
| Project Board | `Project Board.md` | coordination |
| Root Pack | `llms.txt` | routing |
| ObsidianVault tracker | `https://github.com/pixiiidust/ObsidianVault/issues` | coordination |

## Current Narrative / Operating Posture

The project domain is organized as bounded contexts. Active contexts include Critical Ranger FFM, Planned Program Intel, Hermes Mission Control, Pixoid Agent Capability Routes, and Zerg AI Democratization Roadmap. Parked/historical contexts remain useful as references but should not become default work without explicit reprioritization.

Knowledge Pack Routing is currently an architecture workstream under Hermes Mission Control, not a Planned Program Intel deliverable. Planned Program Intel was the first proof/project pack; it is not the default next project unless Jamie says so.

## Project Pack Coverage

| Project/domain | Status | Agent entrypoint |
|---|---|---|
| Hermes Mission Control | active agent-ops hub | `Projects/Hermes Mission Control/llms.txt` |
| Planned Program Intel | active; build queue complete; first KPR proof | `Projects/Planned Program Intel/llms.txt` |
| Critical Ranger FFM | active research/build | `Projects/Critical Ranger FFM/llms.txt` |
| Pixoid Agent Capability Routes | active/completed route governance reference | `Projects/Pixoid Agent Capability Routes/Index.md` |
| Zerg AI Democratization Roadmap | active brainstorm | `Projects/Zerg AI Democratization Roadmap/Index.md` |
| Planned.com Demo | historical | `Projects/Planned/Index.md` |
| myAbode | parked | `Projects/myAbode/Index.md` |
| LeJEPA | concluded/historical | `Projects/LeJEPA/Index.md` |
| EdenOS | parked | `Projects/EdenOS/Index.md` |
| Personal/Pixoid Setup | infrastructure/operator context | `Projects/Personal/Pixoid Setup.md` |
| New PC Build Architecture | backlog | `Projects/New PC Build Architecture/Index.md` |

## Do Not Do

- Do not treat the most visible proof project as the default system goal.
- Do not implement parked/historical projects without explicit reopening.
- Do not trust project status from this pack without checking the listed canonical sources.
- Do not create a project-specific pack unless the project has distinct routing/freshness/ownership needs.
- Do not create runtime automation from this pack.

## Fallback Rules

1. Project has `llms.txt`: load it, then its canonical sources.
2. Project has no `llms.txt`: load `Projects/<Project>/Index.md`.
3. Project has no index: use Projects MOC and Project Board.
4. Current implementation state matters: verify GitHub issues/PRs and repo state live.
5. Project is parked/historical: stop unless Jamie explicitly reopens it.

## Human Surface

- `Maps of Content/Projects MOC.md`
- `Project Board.md`
- project `Index.md` notes

## Maintenance Notes

Update this pack when a project changes active/parked/historical status, when a new project pack is created, or when the default project routing queue changes. Keep project details in project hubs; this pack should stay a routing map.
